task_spec: Design a new customizable dashboard product for multi-provider AI usage observability (Claude + OpenAI) with project-level attribution, cost/token analytics, and Claude memory-file insights.
scope: greenfield project architecture, feature set, data model, UX customization model, phased implementation plan
out-of-scope: writing production code in this step
out-of-scope: committing to one fixed frontend framework before tradeoff review
constraints: MUST: support provider split and per-project attribution for Claude and OpenAI
MUST: include Claude memory-file analytics in dashboard features
MUST: support fully customizable dashboard views/layouts/widgets
SHOULD: preserve local-first/privacy-first operation with optional team sync
SHOULD: keep metrics auditable with freshness and attribution confidence indicators
entry_points: /home/franky/repos/cusage/bin/cusage.mjs - existing parser and cost logic reference
rejected_alternatives: pure stream-first architecture for v1 - dismissed due to high operational burden
single-provider-only analytics - dismissed because user requires Claude and OpenAI comparison
current_understanding: cusage already merges Claude and Codex token/cost telemetry from local sources
codex-lb already exposes request-level logs with model/token/cost/latency but lacks explicit project/provider fields
Claude memory files are available locally and can be analyzed at metadata/churn level
user wants extensive feature research plus final setup/design/roadmap plan
assumptions: initial release can accept estimated cost layer before full invoice reconciliation (M)
project attribution can be bootstrapped via deterministic ladder + confidence scoring (M)
target users include both individual developers and teams/FinOps viewers (H)
dashboard should be web-based with saved views and role presets (M)
invisible_knowledge: token/cost dashboards must distinguish estimated vs billed to avoid false precision
missing attribution and stale data must be visibly surfaced, not silently hidden
customizability needs guardrails (certified metrics, shared view governance) to avoid metric drift
auditability and trust are as important as visual richness
reference_docs: /home/franky/repos/cusage/README.md - cusage data sources and usage model
